add.description: Adds the values in registers rs and rt, storing the result in register rd. Triggers an arithmetic overflow exception if the result exceeds the signed 32-bit integer range.

addi.description: Adds a sign-extended 16-bit immediate value to the contents of register rs and stores the result in register rt. Raises overflow exception on signed overflow.

sub.description: Subtracts the value in register rt from register rs and stores the result in register rd. Triggers an arithmetic overflow exception on signed integer overflow.

mul.description: Multiplies the values in registers rs and rt, storing the lower 32 bits of the result in register rd. A pseudo-instruction in some assemblers that wraps mult and mflo.

and.description: Performs a bitwise AND operation between the values in registers rs and rt, storing the result in register rd for masking and flag extraction operations.

or.description: Performs a bitwise OR operation between the values in registers rs and rt, storing the result in register rd for combining bit flags or setting specific bits.

sll.description: Shifts the value in register rt left by the specified shift amount of 0 to 31 bits, filling vacated positions with zeros and storing the result in register rd.

srl.description: Shifts the value in register rt right by the specified shift amount of 0 to 31 bits, filling vacated positions with zeros as a logical shift and storing result in register rd.

lw.description: Loads a 32-bit word from memory at the address computed by adding the sign-extended offset to register rs, storing the value in register rt. Address must be word-aligned.

sw.description: Stores the 32-bit word from register rt into memory at the address computed by adding the sign-extended offset to register rs. Address must be word-aligned.

lb.description: Loads a byte from memory at the computed address, sign-extends it to 32 bits, and stores the result in register rt. Used for reading signed character data.

sb.description: Stores the least significant byte of register rt into memory at the address computed by adding the sign-extended offset to register rs.

la.description: Pseudo-instruction that loads the 32-bit address of a label into register rt. Typically expands to a lui and ori instruction pair for the upper and lower 16 bits.

li.description: Pseudo-instruction that loads a 32-bit immediate constant into register rt, expanding to lui and ori as needed depending on the magnitude of the value.

beq.description: Branch on Equal compares registers rs and rt, and if they are equal, branches to the target label by adding the sign-extended offset to the program counter.

bne.description: Branch on Not Equal compares registers rs and rt, and if they differ, branches to the target label by adding the sign-extended offset to the program counter.

slt.description: Set on Less Than sets register rd to 1 if the signed value in rs is less than the signed value in rt, otherwise sets rd to 0. Essential for comparison-based branching.

j.description: Unconditional Jump instruction that jumps to the instruction at the specified 26-bit target address, with the upper 4 bits from the current program counter. Used for loops and gotos.

jal.description: Jump and Link saves the return address PC+4 in register $ra and jumps to the target address. The standard instruction for calling subroutines in MIPS assembly programs.

jr.description: Jump Register jumps to the address stored in register rs. Commonly used with $ra to return from a subroutine called by jal, restoring execution to the caller.

syscall.description: Triggers a system call to the operating system or simulator. The service number in $v0 with arguments in $a0-$a3. Common services include 1 for print_int, 4 for print_string, 10 for exit.

$v0.description: Value register 0, used for function return values and syscall service numbers. By convention, integer return values from functions are placed in $v0 before returning.

$a0.description: Argument register 0, used to pass the first argument to subroutines and syscalls. Part of the $a0-$a3 convention for the first four function parameters in MIPS calling convention.

$sp.description: Stack Pointer register, pointing to the top of the runtime stack in memory. Grows downward by convention; functions adjust it with addi $sp, $sp, -N to allocate stack frames.

$ra.description: Return Address register, automatically set by the jal instruction to hold the address of the instruction following the call. Used by jr $ra to return from subroutines.

.data.description: Assembler directive that marks the beginning of the data segment, where variables, strings, and constants are declared using directives like .word, .asciiz, and .byte.

.text.description: Assembler directive that marks the beginning of the code and text segment containing executable instructions. Typically follows the .data section in MIPS assembly programs.

.globl.description: Assembler directive that declares a label as globally visible, making it accessible to the linker and other object files. Commonly used with main as the program entry point.

.asciiz.description: Assembler directive that stores a null-terminated ASCII string in the data segment, automatically appending a zero byte. Used for strings passed to print_string syscall.

.word.description: Assembler directive that stores one or more 32-bit integer values in the data segment, each occupying four consecutive bytes with proper word alignment.
